Functional Unit: 1 m² of floor covering with a reference service life (RSL) of 1 year for specified characteristics application and use areas according to declared use classification ISO 10874.
Life Cycle Scope Description: Cradle to grave and module D (A + B + C + D)
Short Description: Bolon is a Swedish design company that makes innovative flooring solutions for public spaces. It is a third-generation family business run by sisters Annica and Marie Eklund. Under their leadership, Bolon has transformed from a traditional weaving mill into an international design brand with clients in different sectors all over the world. With a strong commitment to sustainability, Bolon designs and manufactures all its products at a facility in Ulricehamn in Sweden. The company is recognized worldwide for its award-winning flooring and its collaborations with some of the world's most acclaimed innovators and creatives.
Application: Suitable for hotels, shops, offices, and high traffic areas, such as public halls.

Embodied Energy (PERT - Renewable).Description: Total renewable primary energy (PERT=RPRE+RPRM or PERE+PERM)

Embodied Energy (PENRT - Non-Renewable).Description: Total non-renewable primary energy (PENRT=NRPRE+NRPRM or PENRE+PENRM)

Acidification Potential (AP).Description: AP (or PA) values for different stages

Material Composition Raw Text: Bolon flooring in tiles is composed by four layers, one weave and three backing layers, giving the product its unique qualities. The weave layer is made of vinyl warp and weft yarns tightly woven together. The variations of the warp and weft yarn together with the different weaving techniques gives Bolon flooring its vast design alternatives. Floors are graded into different classes according to their resistance to wear. For example, they are suitable for hotels, shops, offices, and high traffic areas, such as public halls
Recycled Content Data Text: Recycling is an integral part of our production, the recycled material is self-declared according to ISO 14021. Economic allocation has been used to distribute environmental burdens for recycled PVC and pre-consumer material.
End of Life Plan Data Value: At the end of its life the product is sent to either landfill or incineration with energy recovery.
Certifications List: EN 14041, CE-certified, M1, FloorScore, Green star Best environmental practice PVC
Long Description: All floor manufacturing takes place in Ulricehamn, Sweden. Here we manufacture the thread and the backing, weave the designer surface, and combine all these elements into a high-quality floor. Recycling is an integral part of our production, the recycled material is self-declared according to ISO 14021. The floor is then packed and shipped to customers. Installation is normally made with adhesives. Alternative installation methods are possible within Bolon recommendations. The floor coverings are water resistant and are cleaned using wet methods. Most cleaning needs can be accomplished with a vacuum cleaner, scrubbing brush, water, and a minimal dose of stain remover. At the end of its life the product is sent to either landfill or incineration with energy recovery.

Functional Unit Text: "The production and use of 1 square meter of 20 mm wood flooring for a lifespan of 50 years
LCA Table Context:
The EPD presents LCA results for three wooden floorings: DuoPlank® 20 mm, DuoPlank® 15 mm, and Lite 14mm. However, the document explicitly states on page 4 under "REPRESENTATIVENESS" that "DuoPlank® 20 mm is the reference product" and represents the "worst-case scenario" due to its highest material weight input. Therefore, the LCA data provided in the tables on pages 5, 6, and 7 are representative of the DuoPlank® 20 mm product.

The system boundaries for this EPD are defined as "Cradle-to-Grave and module D". The life cycle stages assessed are A1 (Raw material supply), A2 (Transport of raw materials to manufacturer), A3 (Manufacturing), A4 (Transport to customer), A5 (Installation process), B1 (Use), B2 (Maintenance), C1 (Deconstruction and demolition), C2 (Transport of waste), C3 (Waste processing), C4 (Disposal), and D (Reuse, recovery, recycling potential). Modules B3 (Repair), B4 (Replacement), B5 (Refurbishment), B6 (Operational energy use), and B7 (Operational water use) are explicitly marked as "ND" (Not Declared) in the tables.

The LCA values are structured in tables with rows representing environmental indicators (e.g., GWP-total, ADP-fossil, PERE, FW) and columns representing the different life cycle stages (A1, A2, A3, A1-A3, A4, A5, B1, B2, C1, C2, C3, C4, D).
Specifically:
-   Product Stage (A1-A3): A1 (Raw material supply), A2 (Transport of raw materials to manufacturer), and A3 (Manufacturing) are assessed individually and as a sum (A1-A3).
-   Construction Process Stage (A4-A5): A4 (Transport to customer) and A5 (Installation process) are assessed individually.
-   User Stage (B1-B7): B1 (Use) and B2 (Maintenance) are assessed individually. B3, B4, B5, B6, B7 are "ND" (Not Declared).
-   End of Life Stage (C1-C4): C1 (Deconstruction demolition), C2 (Transport), C3 (Waste processing), and C4 (Disposal) are assessed individually.
-   Benefits and Loads Beyond the System Boundaries (D): Module D is assessed.

Footnotes and assumptions impacting interpretation:
-   Disclaimer [2] on pages 5 and 6 states that "The results of this environmental impact indicator shall be used with care as the uncertainties on these results are high or as there is limited experience with the indicator." This applies to ADP-minerals&metals, ADP-fossil, WDP, ETP-fw, HTP-c, HTP-nc, and SQP.
-   Disclaimer [1] on page 6 for IRP clarifies that "This impact category deals mainly with the eventual impact of low dose ionizing radiation on human health of the nuclear fuel cycle. It does not consider effects due to possible nuclear accidents, occupational exposure nor due to radioactive waste."
-   For B1 (Use), "no impacts related to the use of the floorings are considered."
-   For C1 (Deconstruction and demolition), "The removal of the floorboards from the building is done manually, and therefore, no inputs or outputs are included in this stage."
-   For C4 (Disposal), the result was "adjusted based on the biogenic uptake in module A1" due to the release of biogenic carbon from incinerating the floorings.

Short Description: DuoPlank® 20 mm is an engineered wood flooring for indoor use, featuring an oak or ash top layer and birch plywood. Produced in the Netherlands, it has a 20mm thickness, weighs 13.4 kg/m², and boasts a 50-year service life. It is sold globally and is designed for manual installation and removal.
Long Description: DuoPlank® 20 mm is an engineered wood flooring designed for indoor use, manufactured by Hakwood B.V. in Werkendam, the Netherlands. This product features a 6mm oak or ash top layer, supplemented with 15mm birch plywood, glue, coloring agents, and protective coatings. Each square meter of the product weighs 13.4 kg. It has a reference service life of 50 years, which includes maintenance such as sanding and painting every 10 years. The product is available in Europe, the US, and Asia. Both the installation and removal of the flooring are designed to be done manually, requiring no energy consumption for these stages. The EPD covers a comprehensive Cradle-to-Grave scope, including module D for reuse, recovery, and recycling potential, assessing environmental impacts from raw material extraction to final disposal.

It features an oak wear layer on a pine substrate, finished with multiple layers of UV-cured lacquer for high durability. Manufactured in Poland, it is suitable for residential and commercial interior use, including with underfloor heating systems. The product does not contain hazardous substances above 0.1% by mass.
Long Description: OAK/PINE 15 MM LACQUERED is a premium double-layered engineered wood flooring product from Havwoods' Italian Collection. It consists of a hardwood oak wear layer, typically 3-6 mm thick, permanently bonded to a dimensionally stable pine substrate. The product is factory-finished with multi-stage lacquering processes, including primers, intermediate coats, and a UV-cured top layer, ensuring high surface durability, resistance to mechanical stress, and immediate readiness for use. It is available in semi-matt and extra-matt variants, often brushed and beveled on four sides. This flooring is suitable for interior use in residential, commercial, and public buildings, and its low thermal resistance makes it ideal for underfloor heating systems. The product contributes to indoor environmental quality and acts as a carbon store, and it does not contain REACH SVHC substances above 0.1% by mass.

Long Description: The Pureplank, V Collection 3-layer wooden floorboard (14 mm) is a durable flooring product designed for stability and ease of installation. It features a three-layer real wood cross structure that prevents swelling, squeaking, and splitting, making it resistant to temperature and humidity changes. This floorboard is suitable for installation over underfloor heating systems and can be laid using a glueless floating system with 5Gc lock and click joints, or traditionally by gluing to the subfloor. It requires no additional preservative treatment and is ready for immediate use after installation. The product is also designed for renovation, extending its service life.

It features a net carbon negative backing for cradle-to-gate impacts and incorporates recycled content nylon yarn. Long Description: CQUEST™ BIO MODULAR CARPET TILE is a high-performance modular carpet tile specifically engineered for commercial buildings. It features a unique non-vinyl bio-composite backing, CQuest™ Bio, which is made from bio-resins, bio-fillers, and bio-oils, resulting in a net carbon negative Global Warming Potential (GWP) for cradle-to-gate impacts. The product incorporates recycled content nylon yarn, with 9% post-consumer recycled nylon and 10% pre-consumer recycled nylon, alongside 45% pre-consumer recycled limestone in its secondary backing. Designed for modular installation, it offers a reference service life of 15 years and is intended for reuse and recycling through Interface’s ReEntry program, minimizing end-of-life waste. Its backing is impervious to water, protecting subfloors from leaks and spills, and it meets Class 1 fire rating (ASTM E-648).

The product has a reference service life of 35 years and is certified for indoor air quality.
Long Description: Karndean Designflooring's Van Gogh is a luxury vinyl tile (LVT) flooring product designed for both commercial and residential interior applications. It is available in various designs, formats, and sizes, including tiles and planks. The product is constructed with multiple layers, including a PU protective layer, a clear embossed PVC wear layer, a high-definition photographic layer, and stability and backing layers that incorporate post-industrial recycled material. The primary materials include polyvinyl chloride (PVC), plasticizers, fillers, and various stabilizers and coatings. Manufacturing occurs in China, South Korea, and Taiwan. The product has a reference service life of 35 years and is certified by FloorScore® and Indoor Air Comfort Gold for VOC emissions, ensuring good indoor air quality. At end-of-life, it is assumed to be disposed of in a landfill or via incineration.

Short Description: Karndean Designflooring's KORLOK/VAN GOGH RIGID CORE (6.5MM) is an Engineered Plastic Composite (WPC/EPC) resilient flooring product. It is suitable for both commercial and residential interiors, available in planks, and features a rigid core with an acoustic backing. The product is designed for installation over uneven subfloors and aims to reduce noise transfer. It is disposed of in a landfill at end-of-life.
Long Description: Karndean Designflooring's KORLOK/VAN GOGH RIGID CORE (6.5MM) is an Engineered Plastic Composite (WPC/EPC) flooring product, part of their Rigid Core range. It is suitable for both commercial and residential interiors and is available in various designs, formats, and sizes, including planks. The product is structured with multiple layers, featuring a rigid core for installation over uneven subfloors and a pre-attached acoustic backing for excellent acoustic properties, reducing noise transfer. Regrind material is incorporated into the stability layers. The primary materials include PVC, plasticizers, fillers, stabilizers, pigments, and coatings. Manufacturing occurs in China and Vietnam, involving mixing, extrusion, calendering into sheets, embossing, coating, and cutting into individual planks.

End-of-Life and Disposal: Wallcoverings are subject to waste code 170904 (mixed construction and demolition waste) per the European Waste Catalogue. Short Description: Vinyl based wallcoverings on non-woven backing are decorative interior wall coverings consisting of plasticized PVC on a cellulose/polyester non-woven substrate. Produced by 15 IGI member companies across Europe and the US, they weigh 0.223 kg/m² and have a 10-year reference service life.
Long Description: Vinyl based wallcoverings on non-woven backing, as defined in EN 15102, consist of plasticized PVC (vinyl) applied to a non-woven base substrate composed of a blend of cellulose (80%) and polyester fibres (20%). The product is available with relief or flat vinyl designs and is dimensionally stable to aqueous paste. Unlike conventional wallcovering, it does not require a soak time and can be stripped in its entirety (strippable as per EN 235) when replacement is needed. The declared unit is 1 m² with a grammage of 0.223 kg/m². The weighted average material composition is: Non-Woven 35%, PVC plastisol 32%, Inks 14%, Chemicals and auxiliary materials 11%, and Packaging 8%. It is used for decorative wall design in private or commercial interior spaces. The reference service life is 10 years per the German BNB system. Installation involves applying adhesive to the back of the wallcovering or the substrate using a brush or roller. The EPD is an industry average covering 15 of 67 IGI member companies across Germany, UK, Belgium, Italy, US, and France.

Short Description: The Schüco FWS 50 is an aluminum facade system (3050x3050 mm) designed for building envelopes, offering high thermal insulation and slim sightlines. It integrates windows and doors, with powder-coated or anodized aluminum profiles. The system is largely recyclable, with high rates for aluminum and steel, and thermal recovery for plastics, contributing to a lower environmental impact.
Long Description: The Schüco FWS 50 is a Façade Wall System (FWS) consisting of insulated aluminum composite profiles, designed for use as an external building envelope. This system offers functional advantages combined with architectural and design aspects, featuring high thermal insulation and slender visible widths. Schüco window systems (AWS) and door systems (ADS) can be integrated. Profile surfaces can be anodized, wet-coated, or powder-coated. The declared element, measuring 3050 mm x 3050 mm, includes main profiles, accessories, accessory profiles, gaskets, triple insulating glass, and a powder coating. It is intended for commercial applications, providing light and air exchange. The product is designed for a service life of over 50 years and is free of chromium(VI), heavy metals, and VOCs in its powder coatings.

Short Description: Residential broadloom carpet with virgin Polyester face fiber, featuring SBR latex precoat and woven polypropylene secondary backing. Designed for residential flooring applications with a 10-year reference service life.
Long Description: Residential Broadloom Carpet with Polyester Face Fiber is a tufted broadloom carpet designed for residential settings. The face fiber is made from virgin Polyester produced internally at Shaw through polymerization and extrusion, with face weights ranging from 10.5 to 90 oz per sq. yd., with a weighted average of 38.43 osy. The manufacturing process involves turning the Polyester into yarn through various processes depending on the desired look, then tufting the yarn into a polypropylene primary backing layer, applying an SBR latex precoat to ensure maximum tuft bind, and adding a woven polypropylene secondary backing layer. A topical layer of hydrophobically modified thermoplastic polyester is applied. The product has a total thickness of 9.14mm and product weight of 2.38 kg/m². It is manufactured in Dalton, GA in ISO 9001 & ISO 14001 certified facilities. The product meets Green Label Plus certification for indoor air quality and is rated Radiant Panel Class I for fire performance. Installation requires adhesive application, and maintenance includes regular vacuuming and annual hot water extraction. The reference service life is 10 years.

Long Description: Residential Broadloom Carpet with Nylon-6 Face Fiber is a tufted broadloom carpet designed for residential settings. The face fiber is made from virgin Nylon-6 polymer with face weights ranging from 11 to 100 oz per sq. yd., with a weighted average of 24.68 osy. The manufacturing process involves tufting the yarn into a polypropylene primary backing layer, applying an SBR latex precoat to ensure maximum tuft bind, and adding a woven polypropylene secondary backing layer. A topical layer of methacrylate polymer and hydrophobically modified thermoplastic polyester is applied. The product has a total thickness of 9.4mm and product weight of 1.92 kg/m². It is manufactured in Dalton, GA in ISO 9001 & ISO 14001 certified facilities. The product meets Green Label Plus certification for indoor air quality and is rated Radiant Panel Class I for fire performance. Installation requires adhesive application, and maintenance includes regular vacuuming and annual hot water extraction. The reference service life is 10 years.

Short Description: Acrolon 7300 B65W1301 is a high solids acrylic polyurethane coating by Sherwin-Williams. It serves as a durable finish coat over anti-corrosion systems, offering excellent resistance to atmospheric exposures and maintaining gloss and color in corrosive environments. It is tin-free and silica-free.
Long Description: Acrolon 7300 B65W1301 is an architectural coating manufactured by The Sherwin-Williams Company. It is a high solids acrylic polyurethane coating designed as a durable finish coat over high-performance anti-corrosion systems. This tin-free and silica-free aliphatic polyurethane offers excellent resistance to atmospheric exposures, maintaining gloss and color even in highly corrosive environments. It is formulated for interior or exterior architectural substrates such as drywall, stucco, wood, metal, concrete, and masonry, specifically designed to cover and protect metal architectural surfaces like rebar. The product is manufactured globally and has a market-based lifetime of 10 years.

Short Description: Duraplate 301W N02301W10 is a two-pack modified epoxy architectural coating. It offers low-temperature curing, surface and humidity tolerance, good chemical and abrasion resistance, and edge-retentive properties. Ideal for on-site applications due to its low solvent content, it's designed to protect metal architectural surfaces.
Long Description: Duraplate 301W N02301W10 is an architectural coating manufactured by The Sherwin-Williams Company. It is a low-temperature curing, surface and humidity tolerant two-pack modified epoxy, designed to cover and protect metal architectural substrates like rebar. This product can be applied without dew point restrictions and over wet steel surfaces, and is suitable for steel prepared by hydroblasting, grit blasting, or mechanical tooling. It exhibits good chemical and abrasion resistance, combined with good edge-retentive properties. Its low solvent content makes it ideal for on-site applications. Classified as a primer/other, it falls under the architectural coatings PCR for decorative or protective paints for interior or exterior architectural substrates.

It is a water-based, quick-drying gloss finish or sealercoat designed to cover and protect metal architectural surfaces, such as structural steel.
